$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the funpack (Odin) <-> funpack-mcp (Go) API boundary. Authored by hand; consumed by contract/gen which emits BOTH sides (mcp/internal/contract/*_gen.go and funpack/api_contract.gen.odin). This file owns SHAPE (field names, command/event taxonomy) and the MCP's supported version RANGES; the actual schema VALUES live in the Odin constants (ARTIFACT_SCHEMA_VERSION, INDEX_SCHEMA_VERSION, INTROSPECT_PROTOCOL_VERSION) and are pinned to this contract by a read-only sync test. See ADR 2026-06-15-funpack-api-contract-dual-codegen. Bumping funpack's schema is a deliberate edit of the matching supported range here, never a silent drift.

version_surface.$comment: Shape of `funpack version --json`. The MCP resolver consumes this JSON; it never parses the human `funpack version` report.

schemas.doc: schema-name -> emitted version; an absent key means the build does not expose that schema yet (not an error)

supported.$comment: MCP compat policy: the funpack schema versions this MCP build accepts. Exact-match by default (the §29 Index-Contract discipline). Preflight refuses a PRESENT schema outside its range; an ABSENT schema (e.g. introspect before t-version-line lands) never refuses.

introspect.$comment: Spec §28 introspection contract (runtime -> agent), mirrored from runtime/introspect.odin. A read-only sync test pins protocol_version + commands + events against the Odin source; the Go §28 client (codec/handshake/demux) is generated from here, never re-declaring these.

envelopes.$comment: Three closed message kinds, each a fixed-order field tuple, versioned exact-match (every envelope stamps `v`; a consumer refuses a version or field-shape mismatch rather than best-effort parsing).

request.doc: v=protocol version; id correlates the response; cmd is the command name; args is its argument object

response.doc: ok is the success boolean; exactly one of result (when ok) or error (otherwise) is present — never both, never neither

event.doc: unsolicited push correlated by `event` name (not id); breakpoint_hit and watch_fired carry probe payloads (target and value)

command_groups.$comment: The closed command set, grouped by surface and tagged with its determinism class. observe = non-perturbing (reads canonical chain by default; accepts an optional `branch` arg the generator injects per-class). control = perturbing -> forks a non-warranted branch off a snapshot. Each group's `tool_prefix` projects the MCP tool name: non-empty -> '<prefix>_<command>' (time/inspect/control), empty -> the bare command name (break, self_heal). Each command carries its §28 wire `args` shape (the object inside a request envelope's `args` field), authored by hand here from the runtime source (runtime/introspect_*.odin): the generator PROJECTS these into the MCP tools/list input_schema (a session_id-keyed superset) AND the dispatch hints, so the advertised schema cannot drift from dispatch. An arg's `type` is its JSON-Schema type (string|integer|number|boolean|array|object); `required` marks a mandatory arg; `doc` is the schema description. An empty `args` object means the command takes only the session handle.

until.doc: target tick to run forward to; omitted runs to the last recorded tick (a target behind the cursor is refused — use rewind)

tick.doc: target tick to rewind the cursor to (>= -1, within the recording); restores the nearest snapshot at-or-before and re-folds forward

on_signal.doc: break{on_signal:S} form: pause when a signal of type S is routed during a tick; mutually exclusive with target/body

target.doc: break{when:<pred>} form: the behavior to fold the predicate against; required with body, mutually exclusive with on_signal

body.doc: break{when:<pred>} form: the one-expression predicate node forest over the wire; required with target

target.doc: the behavior to watch; watch{<expr>} fires when the expression value changes across steps

body.doc: the one-expression watched-expression node forest over the wire

handle.doc: the live-probe handle minted by a prior break/watch to remove

tick.doc: the recorded tick whose routed signals to dump
the recorded tick to trace the behavior at

behavior.doc: the behavior name whose per-instance (in -> out) to trace

from.doc: the earlier retained tick of the diff

to.doc: the later retained tick of the diff

tick.doc: the recorded tick to replay the behavior at

behavior.doc: the behavior name to re-run in isolation from its captured inputs

tick.doc: the committed tick whose §20 draw-list to dump (the deterministic, always-serving render projection)
the committed tick to capture as a presented frame (pixels)

include_drawlist.doc: when true, the deterministic §20 draw-list rides along with the pixels (default false: visual-only)

pressed.doc: input records pressed THIS tick (each {player, action})

values.doc: 1D analog records (each {player, action, value} as a Fixed raw-bit string)

axes.doc: 2D analog records (each {player, action, x, y} as Fixed raw-bit strings)

ticks.doc: number of full pipeline ticks to fold the snapshot through (>=1, default 1)

thing.doc: the thing (table) declaring the field to force

value.doc: the new value as a source literal — the same spelling inspect_draw_list / inspect_state / inspect_signals render (a Fixed as a decimal '110.0', a vector as 'Vec2(x=2.0,y=104.0)'), so an observed value pastes straight back; a raw Q32.32 integer is also accepted

instance.doc: the instance id of the row to set (defaults to 0)

thing.doc: the thing (table) to spawn an instance of

fields.doc: field overrides keyed by field name, each a source literal (a Fixed as '110.0', a vector as 'Vec2(x=2.0,y=104.0)') — the spelling the observe surfaces render; a raw Q32.32 integer is also accepted; unspecified fields take the declared default

thing.doc: the thing (table) of the instance to despawn

instance.doc: the instance id of the row to despawn

signal.doc: the signal type name to emit

value.doc: the signal payload as a record source literal of the signal type (e.g. 'Goal(side=Side::Left)'); Fixed/vector fields take their decimal source spelling, the same the observe surfaces render; a raw Q32.32 integer is also accepted

artifact.doc: path to the recompiled artifact to hot-reload the branch onto

tick.doc: the canonical tick to fork at (default: the canonical head); -1 forks post-startup

target.doc: which lineage to make active: 'branch' (default) or 'canonical'

behavior.doc: name of the behavior whose step to capture into a regression test

tick.doc: the recorded tick at which to capture the behavior's (self, resources, inbound signals)

instance.doc: optional Thing id to target one behavior instance; omit to take the first instance in fold order

server_tools.$comment: The SERVER-NATIVE MCP tool surface, distinct from the §28 introspect.command_groups: these tools are backed by in-process compute-halves and the session registry, NOT by §28 wire commands. The generator projects EVERY tool here into the SAME unified funpack.TOOL_SPECS table the §28 commands feed (one Tool_Spec each: name + input_schema args), so the advertised tools/list input_schema cannot drift from dispatch. Tools are grouped by their MCP dispatch FAMILY (the downstream tools/call arm that owns them): `oneshot` = stateless compute tools (build/check/export/test/fmt/warden_*); `docs_health` = the embedded-corpus + liveness tools; `session` = the session-lifecycle tools that drive the server-scoped registry. Each family carries its determinism `class` (the Tool_Spec.class hint) and `session_scoped` (false for every server-native tool — none rides a named §28 session). Unlike a §28 command, a server-native tool has NO wire command and NO injected session_id/branch arg: its `name` IS its dispatch key, and its `args` ARE its full input_schema (each {type, required, doc} mirroring a §28 arg). SOURCE OF TRUTH for each arg shape: the Go tool definitions under mcp/internal/server (BuildInput/CheckInput/FmtInput/TestInput, Warden*Input, DocsGetInput/DocsSearchInput, HealthInput, Session*Input). See ADR 2026-06-18-mcp-server-native-tools-in-contract.

dir.doc: absolute path to the funpack project tree to build (the funpack verb operates on the project at this working directory)

release.doc: build the optimized shippable artifact (passes --release); omit for a fast dev build

dir.doc: absolute path to the funpack project tree to export the shippable (release) artifact for (the funpack verb operates on the project at this working directory)
absolute path to the funpack project tree to check (the funpack verb operates on the project at this working directory)

release.doc: check against the release build configuration (passes --release)

dir.doc: absolute path to the funpack project tree to test (funpack runs every test block in the tree at this working directory)
absolute path to the funpack project tree to format (the funpack verb operates on the project at this working directory)

check.doc: verdict-only mode (passes --check): report whether the tree is formatted via the exit code without rewriting files

dir.doc: project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to query

query.doc: declaration name substring to look up (the warden find positional filter); an empty result means nothing existing to reuse

dir.doc: project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to query

node.doc: optional node name to filter the graph to one node's edges; omit for the full dependency graph

dir.doc: project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to query for typed holes
project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to query for debug probes
project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to query for declarations tagged as debt
project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to query for the registered governance tags
project directory whose committed .funpack/index.ndjson to project the pipeline from

anchor.doc: stable corpus anchor of the section to fetch (e.g. an engine/<module>#<decl> or <file>#<heading-slug> anchor)

query.doc: search query: an engine symbol/identifier (e.g. world.resolve, @stub) for a declaration, or natural language (e.g. how does determinism work) for a concept

limit.doc: maximum hits to return; omit or <= 0 for the default (10), capped at 50

artifact.doc: path to the built funpack game artifact for the attach session to load

session_id.doc: opaque id of the session to close, as returned by session_start
